revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Aichi Gokoku Shrine]][[fr:Aichi Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:愛知縣護國神社]]
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagoya, Japan|qq=}}{{Referencesの明記|date=2015th year 1月18日 (日) 13:53 (UTC)|qq=}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = {{nihongo|'''Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine'''|愛知縣護國神社|qq=}} | image = Gokoku Shrine.JPG | caption = Main gate of the Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|10|46|N|136|53|59|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline|qq=}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Nagoya Castle]], in central Nagoya, Japan | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Aichiken-Gokoku-Jinja-1.jpg|250px|Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine]] {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=15|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|qq=}} | location = 1-7-3 Sannomaru, Naka-ku, Nagoya, Aichi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|35|10|46|N|136|53|59|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title|qq=}} | deity = Over 93,000 spirits of Aichi Prefecture | type = Shrine designated by the Minister of Home Affairs, Beppyo Shrine | founded = 1869 (Meiji 2) | festival = April 28-30, October 28-30 }} [[File:Gokoku Shrine.JPG|250px|thumb|right|Torii (April 2012)]] '''Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine''' (愛知縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sannomaru]], {{ill|Naka-ku, Nagoya|en|Naka-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1207921|lt=Naka-ku, Nagoya|1=Naka-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. The shrine is currently listed as a Beppyo Shrine under the jurisdiction of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It enshrines over 93,000 spirits from Aichi Prefecture who died in wars from the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} to {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. == History == In {{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}} (Meiji 1), {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|en|Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|qid=Q3530497|lt=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu|1=Tokugawa Yoshikatsu}}, the [[daimyo]] of the [[Owari Domain]], enshrined the spirits of 25 domain warriors who died in the {{ill|Boshin War|en|Boshin War|qid=Q309184|lt=Boshin War|1=Boshin War}} at Kawana Hill in [[:en:Showa-ku, Nagoya|Showa-ku, Nagoya]]. The following year, in May {{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), he constructed a shrine named "Seichusha" to house these spirits. In {{ill|1875|en|1875|qid=Q7764|lt=1875|1=1875}} (Meiji 8), the shrine was renamed "Shokonsha," and in {{ill|1901|en|1901|qid=Q2035|lt=1901|1=1901}} (Meiji 34), it was designated as an official Shokonsha by the government. In {{ill|1918|en|1918|qid=Q2094|lt=1918|1=1918}} (Taisho 7), the shrine was moved to the former Jōhoku training ground (now [[:en:Meijo Park|Meijo Park]] North Garden) and then relocated again in {{ill|1935|en|1935|qid=Q18658|lt=1935|1=1935}} (Showa 10) to its current location. In {{ill|1939|en|1939|qid=Q18639|lt=1939|1=1939}} (Showa 14), it was renamed Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine. During the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}, the shrine was completely destroyed in the [[Nagoya Air Raid]] on March 19, {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Showa 20). After World War II, the shrine was temporarily renamed "Aichi Shrine," but in {{ill|1955|en|1955|qid=Q18577|lt=1955|1=1955}} (Showa 30), it reverted to its original name. It has been listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Deities == * Main deities - Over 93,000 spirits from Aichi Prefecture. == Grounds == {{columns-list|colwidth=15em| * Reiden (Hall of Spirits) * Honden (main hall) - established in {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}} (Showa 33) November. * Haiden (Worship Hall) - established in {{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}} (Showa 33) November. * Maiden (Dance Hall) - established in {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}} (Heisei 10) March. * Shinmon (God Gate) - established in {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}} (Heisei 10) March. * Kairo (Corridor) - established in {{ill|1998|en|1998|qid=Q2089|lt=1998|1=1998}} (Heisei 10) March. * Shamusho (Office) - Built in {{ill|1982|en|1982|qid=Q2436|lt=1982|1=1982}} (Showa 57) October. * Monuments for fallen police officers and firefighters. * Shōwakan. * Statue of a special attack warrior - established in {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}} (Heisei 20) April. * Monument to the Cherry Blossoms of Ayuchi (Manchuria 2603rd Unit). * Kensui Statue. * Monument of successive priests and chief priests. * Monument commemorating the world tour of the Nippon. * Monument to the [[:en:Showa Emperor|Showa Emperor]]'s poetry (written by chamberlain [[:en:Tokugawa Yoshihiro|Tokugawa Yoshihiro]]). * Shōwa no Mori Monument. * Monument for the 3rd Transport Regiment. * Memorial for the Palau Navy Unit. * Memorial for the Battleship {{ill|Yamato|en|Yamato|qid=Q217499|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato}}. * Memorial for Naval Reserve Flying Students. * Yasuragi Monument (51st Independent Transport Battalion). * Monument for the faithful who died in battle (Boshin War, Owari Domain). * Memorial for the 1st and 2nd Independent Artillery Regiments. * Monument for the artillery soldiers' loyalty (14th Independent Artillery Battalion). * Heart Tower (31st Division Medical Unit). * Monuments for the {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|qid=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War|1=Russo-Japanese War}}. * Monuments for the {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|qid=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War|1=First Sino-Japanese War}} (moved from {{ill|en:Liaodong Peninsula|en|en:Liaodong Peninsula|qid=Q326917|lt=en:Liaodong Peninsula|1=Liaodong Peninsula}}). }} == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Related Articles == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] == External Links == {{osm box|w|188861726|qq=}} * {{official|qq=}} * {{Youtube|channel=UCspQHAyttJIUcsUxZ7fDjWQ|qq=}} * {{Commonscat-inline|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}} ==External links== {{Commons category-inline}} * [http://www.aichi-gokoku.or.jp/ Homepage of the Aichi Prefecture Gokoku Shrine] {{Gokoku Shrines|qq=}} {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{Beppyo Shrines|qq=}} \\
